nodes.doc: Regenerates the Go host bindings (std -> host/gen) from std.Module declarations.

nodes.label: build
nodes.doc: Compiles one artifact: the host binary, or the container image under the `container` charm.

nodes.label: build_date
nodes.doc: Commit record date (RFC3339) for reproducible builds; empty => "unknown".

nodes.label: build_release_variant
nodes.doc: Builds one (goos, goarch) release binary and archives it into dist/, writing a standard one-line <asset>.sha256 sidecar alongside it (sha256sum-compatible format). No cross-job aggregation needed: CD's publish job later does `cat dist/*.sha256 > dist/SHA256SUMS`, a plain shell one-liner, not a magus target. release-build's cgo and static (charm) modes both call this, differing only in CGO_ENABLED and the asset suffix.

nodes.label: build_variant
nodes.doc: Builds one image variant; publish => multi-arch --push + cosign-sign, else single-arch --load.

nodes.label: buzz_check
nodes.doc: Type-checks the standalone Buzz with the upstream `buzz` toolchain (--check).

nodes.label: buzz_run
nodes.doc: Type-checks the standalone Buzz with magus's own embedded engine ($MAGUS buzz).

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: Runs lint, build, test, and the coverage-badge freshness gate read-only; the affected/pipeline anchor. coverage drift-gates assets/coverage.svg, so a stale badge fails ci instead of rotting silently.

nodes.label: ci_shard
nodes.doc: Translates a `magus affected --plan` (read on stdin) into GitHub Actions shard-matrix outputs; the gha charm writes $GITHUB_OUTPUT, otherwise the matrix is only previewed.

nodes.label: config_generate
nodes.doc: Regenerates the CLI config-flag plumbing (cmd/magus/gen) from internal/config/config.go.

nodes.label: coverage
nodes.doc: coverage runs the suite with per-package coverage and rewrites the assets/coverage.svg badge. Like `generate`, it then drift-gates: a clean checkout only goes dirty if the committed badge was stale, so `ci` (which depends on this) fails when the badge was not refreshed and committed. Run the `rw` charm (`coverage:rw`) to keep the new badge instead.  Per-package, not -coverpkg=./...: cross-package instrumentation rewrites std/*.go with counter statements, which shifts the source line numbers the module-doc g

nodes.label: docs_generate
nodes.doc: Regenerates the committed docs Markdown (module reference + man pages).

nodes.label: format
nodes.doc: Regenerates, then formats Go, tidies `go.mod`, and prettifies the docs.

nodes.label: generate
nodes.doc: Regenerates every *-generate sibling, then gates on drift (exclusive, scoped to cwd). Drift = regeneration dirtied a previously-clean tree, via vcs.isDirty (not a raw git/porcelain shell-out), so uncommitted-but-current generated files pass; only a regeneration that changes a clean file fails (a generator input changed but its output was not re-run and committed).

nodes.label: go_build
nodes.doc: Compiles the version-stamped magus binary.

nodes.label: image_build
nodes.doc: Under the cd charm, build+push+sign both static and CGO images unconditionally. Otherwise loads one single-arch image locally: CGO/glibc (Dockerfile) by default, or the static/no-libc variant (Dockerfile.static) under the `static` charm — the same default-cgo/opt-in-static convention as release_build() in this file.

nodes.label: image_scan
nodes.doc: Scans the image with trivy; the rw charm writes SARIF and gates on HIGH/CRITICAL.

nodes.label: lint
nodes.doc: Formats first, then golangci-lint, go vet, govulncheck, markdownlint. golangci-lint and govulncheck run via os.exec to pass -modfile=go.tool.mod (their deps live there, not in the library go.mod).

nodes.label: man_generate
nodes.doc: Renders the man pages (roff) into manpage/gen.

nodes.label: md_generate
nodes.doc: Renders MAGUS.md via `magus describe graph`.

nodes.label: postflight
nodes.doc: Renders the insight report (hotspots, affinity, ownership, trend) to stdout and, in GitHub Actions, appends it to the job step summary. The final CI step; never gates.

nodes.label: preflight
nodes.doc: Gates the build on workspace health by running `magus doctor`.

nodes.label: release_build
nodes.doc: Builds one release binary for one platform. Default is dynamically-linked (cgo): internal/codec's zstd/xz fast path needs a real C toolchain + libzstd/liblzma for whatever (goos, goarch) you ask for, so cross-compiling this variant only works if you already have that toolchain around — CD never does, sticking to each native job's own platform. The `static` charm (`magus run release-build:static`) switches to a CGO_ENABLED=0 build instead: pure Go cross-compiles trivially, so unlike the cgo default it's ch

nodes.label: release_sign
nodes.doc: Signs dist/SHA256SUMS with the Ed25519 key in the MAGUS_SIGNING_KEY secret (see cmd/magus-utils/sign.go), then self-verifies the signature against the embedded release pubkey (internal/releasekey) before the release goes out — a cheap regression guard, safe to run here (unlike setup-magus, which can't depend on the magus source tree since it's reused by arbitrary external repos).

nodes.label: release_target_platform
nodes.doc: Resolves which platform to build: the explicit (goos, goarch) args when given, else the current runner's own native platform. So a bare `magus run release-build` (with or without the static charm) builds exactly one binary — the one you're standing on — never a silent "all 5" surprise.

nodes.label: spells_generate
nodes.doc: Regenerates the compiled built-in spell bytecode (internal/spell/gen).

nodes.label: test
nodes.doc: Formats first, then runs the Go test suite.

nodes.label: total_coverage
nodes.doc: Renders a Go coverage badge locally (no third-party service, no network).

nodes.label: watch
nodes.doc: Rebuilds on every debounced change until interrupted; fs.watch BLOCKS, try/catch keeps it alive.

nodes.label: amzTimestamp
nodes.doc: amzTimestamp formats the current UTC time as a SigV4 x-amz-date (YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ), handing the calendar math to the host time module (Go's time.Format under the hood). os.time() returns epoch millis as a double — exactly what the time module speaks — so it flows straight through with no conversion.

nodes.label: cache_key
nodes.doc: cache_key derives the object key from the spec hash (already unique per project; hex, so URL-safe — no extra encoding needed in the canonical URI). NOTE: cache-key format differs from spells/github/actions/spell.buzz on purpose: S3 folds CACHE_VERSION into the key string ("magus-<hash>-<version>"), while GHA keeps the version in a separate `version` request field. Do NOT change the emitted string here — it would invalidate every existing S3 artifact. Track the inconsistency for follow-up only.

nodes.label: deleteObject
nodes.doc: deleteObject removes one object by key (S3 DeleteObject, path-style). DeleteObject is idempotent, returning 204 No Content whether or not the key existed.

nodes.label: enabled
nodes.doc: enabled gates the backend on having credentials and a bucket. The cache probes this once and skips fetch/push entirely when false — no remote calls.

nodes.label: get_artifact
nodes.doc: get_artifact downloads the object for io.hash into io.dest. true on a hit (200), false on a miss (404) or when not configured.

nodes.label: getenv
nodes.doc: getenv reads one environment variable, returning "" when unset. NOTE: byte-identical to the getenv in spells/github/actions/spell.buzz. The duplication is intentional/temporary: there is no shared magus/... buzz helper module for it yet (extracting one needs the embed+register plumbing magus/charm uses). Track for follow-up; keep the two copies in sync.

nodes.label: hostOf
nodes.doc: hostOf extracts the Host header value (authority) from a base URL. SigV4 signs the host header, and the server recomputes the signature against the Host it receives — which Go derives from the URL — so this must equal the URL authority.

nodes.label: listPage
nodes.doc: listPage fetches one page of the bucket listing (S3 ListObjects v1, path-style) for objects under the magus- prefix, starting after marker (""=from the start). Cache keys are hex + literal — all RFC-3986-unreserved — so neither prefix nor marker needs percent-encoding, and the canonical query string (keys sorted: marker before prefix) is byte-identical to the one on the URL. Returns the raw XML body, or "" on a non-200.

nodes.label: listing
nodes.doc: listing is a fiber generator over every magus- object in the bucket. Each resume yields the next {key, lastModified} object, transparently fetching the next S3 page when the current one drains — marker pagination stays hidden behind the coroutine. It returns null when the listing is exhausted; since a real artifact is never null, the consumer drains it with a resume-until-null loop. The bucket's LastModified is the S3-side mtime, so no per-object HEAD is needed.

nodes.label: prune
nodes.doc: prune evicts cache objects per a retention policy, enumerating them through the listing fiber. Inputs arrive via cb: older_than_secs : int  — evict objects older than this many seconds (0 = off) keep_last       : int  — keep only the newest N objects (0 = off) dry_run         : bool — report intended deletions without performing them The two bounds are additive — an object is doomed if it trips either — and the doomed set is keyed by object key so the overlap never double-deletes. Returns true onc

nodes.label: put_artifact
nodes.doc: put_artifact uploads the file at io.src as the object for io.hash. true on a 200 store, false otherwise. The payload hash is the file's SHA-256 (S3 verifies it against x-amz-content-sha256).

nodes.label: sign
nodes.doc: sign builds the signed request headers for an S3 path-style request. path is the canonical URI ("/bucket/key"); query is the canonical query string (keys sorted, "" for none); payloadHash is the hex SHA-256 of the body (EMPTY_SHA256 for a bodyless GET/DELETE). Returns the header map to hand extra/http.

nodes.label: shellcheck
nodes.doc: One shellcheck invocation over every shell source: find feeds xargs with NUL separators, and -r skips running shellcheck on an empty set.

nodes.label: bufBreaking
nodes.doc: breaking checks the current schema against a baseline for backward-incompatible changes (wire and JSON compatibility). It defaults to comparing against the main branch, buf's standard CI pattern; point it elsewhere with a function target when a repo uses a different default branch or an image baseline. This is the protobuf analogue of an API-contract gate: compose it into `lint` so a breaking .proto change fails the same read-only stage as go-vet and golangci-lint. The gha charm swaps buf's reporter to GitH

nodes.label: bufFormat
nodes.doc: format checks by default (--exit-code fails CI when files would change; the write charm applies the formatting in place.

nodes.label: mgs_listProvidedGlobs
nodes.doc: Generated code buf writes. Adjust to match the `out:` directories in your buf.gen.yaml; "gen/**" is the buf convention.

nodes.label: buzzCheck
nodes.doc: check type-checks every Buzz source without running it (buzz --check). buzz takes one script per invocation, so find feeds xargs one file at a time (-n1; -print0 pairs with -0 for safe paths, and -r skips an empty set — the same -print0 | xargs pattern the bash spell uses for shellcheck.

nodes.label: buzzTest
nodes.doc: test runs each source's Buzz `test {}` blocks (buzz --test).

nodes.label: magusBuzz
nodes.doc: magus-buzz executes each source through `magus buzz`, magus's own embedded Buzz engine. It has no check-only mode — executing a file compiles, type-checks, and runs it — so this is the runtime sibling of `buzz-check`. It invokes "$MAGUS": magus exports MAGUS (the running binary's path, à la GNU Make's $(MAKE)) into every spell subprocess, so this resolves to the current magus even uninstalled / under `go run`, with no dependence on PATH. (A bare $MAGUS, not ${MAGUS:-magus}: Buzz reads {...} in a string

nodes.label: sign
nodes.doc: --yes skips the interactive transparency-log confirmation so signing/attesting runs unattended; the caller appends the target reference and flags.

nodes.label: cache_key
nodes.doc: cache_key derives the cache key from the spec hash. The hash already folds in the project path (see cache/hash.go), so it is unique per project and needs no extra namespacing — and being hex, it is URL-safe. NOTE: cache-key format differs from spells/aws/s3-cache/spell.buzz on purpose: GHA keeps CACHE_VERSION in a separate `version` request field, while S3 folds it into the key string. Do NOT change the emitted string here — it would invalidate every existing GHA cache entry. Track the inconsistency for

nodes.label: enabled
nodes.doc: enabled gates the whole backend on running under GitHub Actions. The cache probes this once and skips fetch/push entirely when false, so wiring this spell in a magusfile is a no-op everywhere except GHA — no remote calls, no exports.

nodes.label: get_artifact
nodes.doc: get_artifact downloads the cache artifact for payload.hash into payload.dest. Returns true on a hit (dest written), false on a miss or when not running under Actions.

nodes.label: getenv
nodes.doc: getenv reads one environment variable, returning "" when unset (os.env returns null for an unset var; the callers below test against ""). NOTE: byte-identical to the getenv in spells/aws/s3-cache/spell.buzz. The duplication is intentional/temporary: there is no shared magus/... buzz helper module for it yet (extracting one needs the embed+register plumbing magus/charm uses). Track for follow-up; keep the two copies in sync.

nodes.label: put_artifact
nodes.doc: put_artifact uploads the artifact at payload.src for payload.hash. Returns true on success or when another runner already stored the same key, else false.

nodes.label: rpc
nodes.doc: rpc POSTs a JSON Twirp request to the named CacheService method and returns the host http response map ({status, body}).

nodes.label: goModTidy
nodes.doc: tidy checks by default (--diff exits non-zero if go.mod/go.sum need changes — safe for CI gating); the write charm applies the changes.

nodes.label: goTest
nodes.doc: The cd charm instruments the run with an atomic-mode coverage profile written to coverage.out — the deliverable a CD pipeline ships to a coverage service (e.g. Coveralls). `magus run go::go-test:cd` (or ci:cd) emits the profile.

nodes.label: goVulncheck
nodes.doc: Scans for known vulnerabilities in the module's call graph. Command as a `go tool` (like golangci-lint) so it resolves from go.mod's tool block, not PATH.

nodes.label: uvBuild
nodes.doc: build/clean are uv's own subcommands; pytest and ruff are tools uv merely runs, so they are named after the tool (pytest, ruff-check), not the `uv run` wrapper.

nodes.label: preflight
nodes.doc: preflight is a no-op marker op (no command).

nodes.label: build_graph_explorer
nodes.doc: build_graph_explorer bundles js/graph-explorer.js (d3-force + d3-zoom) into the committed website/gen/graph/explorer.js - the graph analog of build_playground_editor. The /graph/ page loads it to lay out and render the knowledge graph on a canvas. Run by hand when js/graph-explorer.js or the pinned d3 versions change, then commit explorer.js. Same npm ci + esbuild path.

nodes.label: build_playground
nodes.doc: build-playground rebuilds the WebAssembly interpreter the playground page loads: TinyGo compiles ../cmd/buzz-playground straight to website/gen/playground/buzz.wasm and the matching wasm_exec.js glue is copied beside it. Both are committed (like default.pgo), so CI — which has no TinyGo — never rebuilds them. Building directly into gen/ (rather than a separate website/playground/ source dir the render would then copy) avoids keeping two committed copies of the same artifact in sync; gen/ is itself commi

nodes.label: build_playground_editor
nodes.doc: build_playground_editor bundles the vendored CodeMirror editor into the committed website/gen/playground/editor.js — the editor analog of build_playground's committed buzz.wasm. The playground page loads this bundle to upgrade its textarea into a CodeMirror editor with diagnostics, autocompletion, and hover (driven by the wasm language service, globalThis.buzz). Run by hand when js/editor.js or the pinned CodeMirror versions change, then commit editor.js. npm ci restores the build-only deps from package-l

nodes.label: buzz_test
nodes.doc: buzz-test runs scribe's unit tests (the `test "..." {}` blocks in scribe.buzz) through `magus buzz`, in --embedded mode with the pure magus modules registered so scribe's markdown/encoding imports resolve. magus.cmd raises on a non-zero exit, so a failing assertion fails this target.

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: 'ci' is the conventional anchor `magus affected ci` keys off; it fans out lint/build/test, each of which waits for the render via generate.

nodes.label: generate
nodes.doc: generate renders the site and refreshes MAGUS.md, then gates on drift: a clean checkout only goes dirty when gen/ or MAGUS.md actually changed (i.e. a doc edit or target change wasn't re-rendered and committed). Run the `rw` charm (`generate:rw`) to keep the result instead of gating.

nodes.label: md_generate
nodes.doc: md-generate renders MAGUS.md (the target catalog + dependency graph) via `magus describe graph`, parsed statically from this magusfile so it stays in lockstep with the project's targets. Re-run after editing targets and commit: `magus run md-generate site`.

nodes.label: serve
nodes.doc: serve watches ../docs and re-renders gen/ on change — handy for local docs work. fs.watch BLOCKS until interrupted (Ctrl-C); the output lives outside the watched tree (under gen), so a render can't retrigger the watch. try/catch keeps a bad render from ending the watch; returning false keeps watching.

nodes.label: abandonedLinkCheck
nodes.doc: abandonedLinkCheck is the parity gate: every URL the site has shipped before (recorded in URL_LOCK) must still resolve, so a renamed or deleted page can't silently start 404-ing on inbound links and bookmarks. To retire a URL on purpose, either add a redirect (frontmatter `aliases:`, whose stub keeps the old URL resolving) or list it in retired-urls.txt. Runs every render; the lock only grows (updateUrlLock, under the rw charm).

nodes.label: buildCommits
nodes.doc: buildCommits parses one git-log pass: - metaCommits: path -> "full|short|stamp" of the FIRST (newest) sighting. Drives the always-visible "Last updated" footer line. - metaHistory: path -> list of up to HISTORY_MAX "full|short|stamp|subject" entries in newest-first order. Drives the collapsed WS R history panel. The log prints "SUBJ:<one-line subject>" after each ">>>" header so the two fields ride together without a second git spawn.

nodes.label: capitalizeFirst
nodes.doc: dirLabel maps a docs subdirectory name to its breadcrumb label. cap upper-cases the first character of a slug for a breadcrumb label.

nodes.label: checkPathExists
nodes.doc: checkPathExists returns true when target (gen-root-relative, no leading slash) resolves to a real file under gen/. Directory refs (URL ending in "/" or a plain slugged path) resolve to their index.html; assets pass through verbatim.

nodes.label: climb
nodes.doc: climb builds n repetitions of "../".

nodes.label: codeTeaser
nodes.doc: codeTeaser pulls a short code preview from a tour example: skip the leading comment block and blank lines, then take the first several real lines. Gives each card a code snippet at a glance instead of the descriptive header comment.

nodes.label: collapseWs
nodes.doc: collapseWs turns any run of whitespace (newlines/tabs/spaces goldmark left in the markup) into single spaces, so a derived description reads as one clean line.

nodes.label: decodeEntities
nodes.doc: decodeEntities turns the HTML entities goldmark produces back into their characters for a plain-text description. & is decoded LAST so an entity like "&lt;" does not get double-decoded into "<".

nodes.label: deriveDescription
nodes.doc: deriveDescription is the belt-and-suspenders page description: the first substantive paragraph of the rendered HTML, tags stripped, whitespace collapsed, entities decoded, truncated on a word boundary. A leading image-only paragraph (the hero) yields empty text and is skipped. "" when no paragraph has text, so the caller can fall back to SITE_DESC. Runs before escapeAttr re-encodes, so the decode here avoids the double-encoding the plan's risk register flags.

nodes.label: deriveTitle
nodes.doc: deriveTitle reads a page title from the first H1, stripping the code backticks a markdown heading like `# `fs`` carries so the title reads cleanly as plain text in <title>, the breadcrumb, the pager, and the search index. (Frontmatter titles bypass this and are used verbatim.)

nodes.label: isDirectory
nodes.doc: True if path is a directory.

nodes.label: isGenerated
nodes.doc: isGenerated reports whether a page is mechanically generated rather than hand- written. Generated references live under three fixed docs/ subtrees that the doc generators overwrite wholesale: modules/ (cmd/magus-docs), spells/*.md (cmd/magus-spelldocs; the spells.md landing itself is hand-written), and manpage/ (cmd/magus-manpage). srcRel is the docs/-prefixed source path. Kept as a path convention because the generated .md carries no frontmatter marker.

nodes.label: llmsIndexBody
nodes.doc: llmsIndexBody builds the gen/llms.txt document (the llms.txt convention): an H1, a one-line summary, then every docs page as "- [title](base+url): description", sorted by URL for a byte-stable artifact like search-index.json. base is the link prefix (SITE_URL for absolute links, else "/"). Each linked page has its Markdown source at <url>index.md (emitted next to the HTML by renderFile), so an agent can fetch clean Markdown per page. Kept pure so a unit test can pin the exact output.

nodes.label: lookupTitle
nodes.doc: lookupTitle returns the title of the docs page at a clean gen-root URL (e.g. "modules/" -> "magus stdlib"), or "" when no docs page owns that URL. Used by renderBreadcrumb so an intermediate crumb reads as its destination page's real title.

nodes.label: newCleanDirectory
nodes.doc: rewriteLinks applies rewriteHref to every href="..." in a rendered content fragment. It runs on the markdown-rendered body only (before the page template wraps it), so the nav/breadcrumb/pager hrefs — built separately with clean URLs — are never double-processed. oldDir/newDir are the page's old flat directory and new clean directory (both gen-root-relative, "" for the home page). newCleanDirectory gives a docs page's directory after the directory-index move - its clean URL without the trailing slash -

nodes.label: pageKey
nodes.doc: pageKey normalizes a scanReferencesInHTML result (a gen-root-relative path with no trailing slash - "spells/go", "index.html", "assets/x.png") to a page key: "" for the home page, else the directory that holds the page's index.html. Asset paths pass through unchanged and simply never match a real page key.

nodes.label: pageType
nodes.doc: pageType reads the frontmatter `page_type:` marker (empty when absent). The one value that matters today is "overview": a landing/index page whose job is to orient and route to others (the documentation index, a section index like spells.md or modules/, a diagnostics-family README). Overview pages are the hubs the Related ranking favors and the roots the reachability gate trusts, so declaring the type in frontmatter beats guessing it from a URL shape.

nodes.label: parseChangelog
nodes.doc: parseChangelog reads CHANGELOG.md (Keep-a-Changelog form) and extracts every "## [<version>] - <date>" heading and its body. "[Unreleased]" is skipped: the feed is for released versions only. Deterministic: reads a committed file, no network. Ordering follows the file, which authors keep newest-first.

nodes.label: parseTags
nodes.doc: parseTags reads the frontmatter `tags:` list from raw markdown as [str], empty when the field is absent. The one place tags are extracted so the rendered chips and the search index draw from the same source (markdown.frontmatter returns the YAML as JSON; serialize navigates it).

nodes.label: playgroundLink
nodes.doc: playgroundLink deep-links src into the playground editor. The raw source is url-safe base64 (unpadded, matching js/run-example.js and the wasm's applyHashSource decoder) in the #source= fragment, so the code stays client-side (never hits the server/CDN).

nodes.label: pruneStaleOutputs
nodes.doc: pruneStaleOutputs deletes gen/ output left behind by a source that no longer produces it - a renamed/removed docs page, a deleted blog post, a dropped frontmatter alias, a release that fell out of CHANGELOG.md. gen/ is no longer wiped before each render (see the comment in site_render), so this is what keeps it from silently accumulating orphans instead: walk what is really on disk, and for every "<dir>/index.html" NOT in writtenPaths (this render's actual output) and not in alwaysKeep, delete the whole dir

nodes.label: readURLSet
nodes.doc: readURLSet reads a newline-delimited URL file into a set, skipping blanks and "#" comments. Missing file -> empty set (the bootstrap case for URL_LOCK).

nodes.label: readingTime
nodes.doc: readingTime estimates minutes-to-read by weighting content by type rather than blindly counting words: prose reads at 220 wpm, but a fenced code block is parsed line by line (not skimmed like prose) and an image or diagram costs a few seconds of looking. So a page that is mostly code or figures gets a truer estimate than a raw word count, which would badly under- or over-shoot it. Frontmatter is stripped so YAML doesn't skew the count. Returns "" for pages too short to be worth labeling. Deterministic: a pu

nodes.label: render404
nodes.doc: render404 writes gen/404.html - the themed "warped off the map" page GitHub Pages serves for any unmatched path. GitHub Pages keeps the deep 404 URL in the address bar, so the site's ordinary relative paths would break here; this page therefore uses root-absolute paths (/<basePath>/site.css, /<basePath>/ main.js, etc.) - the ONE deliberate exception to the relative-link scheme in WS0b. basePath is derived from SITE_URL (e.g. https://eli.gladman.cc/magus/ -> "/magus/"); when SITE_URL is empty, fall back to "

nodes.label: renderAnnouncementBar
nodes.doc: renderAnnouncementBar renders a dismissible top strip announcing the newest shipped release, or "" when none has shipped yet (so it stays dormant until then). The version comes from parseChangelog (the committed CHANGELOG), so it is drift-stable; announcement.js hides it once the reader dismisses that version and re-shows it on the next release. The GitHub tag link mirrors the releases Atom feed's convention.

nodes.label: renderAutoindex
nodes.doc: autoindex renders one Apache-`mod_autoindex`-styled listing at path (a gen- root-relative dir, without trailing slash; "" for the root of the /public/ tree, "atom" or "release" for a subdir). entries is a list of records with {name, href, size?, modified?, desc}. Every level brackets the listing with a leading and trailing rule, like a real mirror index. Themed with Pico via the shared renderPage() template (no bespoke color palette), so light/dark follow the site theme. rootDepth is how many "../" the call

nodes.label: renderBlogAtom
nodes.doc: renderBlogAtom writes gen/public/atom/blog.atom.xml: an Atom 1.0 feed of site.blogPosts, newest first. Per <entry>: title, link (absolute), id (same URL), updated (post date), summary (description), author. Feed-level updated = newest post's date. Gated on SITE_URL so it can ship dark until the host is confirmed.

nodes.label: renderBlogIndex
nodes.doc: renderBlogIndex writes gen/blog/index.html, a reverse-chronological list of the collected blog posts. It runs AFTER renderBlogPost has populated site.blogPosts for every post in the walk. The listing is a plain <ul> under the shared renderPage() template, so it inherits nav/breadcrumb/footer for free.

nodes.label: renderBlogPager
nodes.doc: renderBlogPager renders the previous/next links between posts, ordered by date (newest to oldest so "next" is older and "prev" is newer, matching timeline intuition). Falls back to filename order when a post lacks a date. Reuses renderPagerLink's climb-relative sibling link form.

nodes.label: renderBlogPost
nodes.doc: renderBlogPost renders one blog/<file>.md as its own directory-index page. base is the file's basename (e.g. "2026-07-01-hello-blog.md"); the written URL is date- scoped, blog/<YYYY>/<MM>/<DD>/<slug>/ (via blogURLPath, from the filename's date prefix), so permalinks sort by date and never collide across years. Post metadata (title/date/description) is captured into site.blogPosts for the index page (and, later, the Atom feed).

nodes.label: renderCommitFooter
nodes.doc: Footer crediting the last commit that touched a page's source (a magus/-relative path), falling back to HEAD for a not-yet-committed file. Keying off the file's own history means a page changes when its source does, not when any commit lands.

nodes.label: renderDevelopment
nodes.doc: renderDevelopment writes the /development/ contributor section: one themed page per project rendering that project's committed MAGUS.md (the target catalog + run-order graph md_generate produces), plus a /development/ landing that links straight to them. There is no separate projects index; the retired /development/projects/ URLs redirect to their flattened locations. Projects are discovered as site.root plus any immediate subdir carrying a MAGUS.md; fs.list is sorted, so the set and its ordering are byte-s

nodes.label: renderDevelopmentPage
nodes.doc: renderDevelopmentPage renders one /development/ page from markdown through the shared renderPage() pipeline (nav, TOC, theme, search index), skipping the doc-only chrome renderPage() already strips for this srcRel prefix (provenance, edit link, nav highlight). url ends in "/"; srcRel uses the "<dir>/_index.md" marker convention so rootPrefix/renderBreadcrumb derive the right depth and trail.

nodes.label: renderDevelopmentPageRaw
nodes.doc: renderDevelopmentPageRaw is renderDevelopmentPage for pages whose body is already final HTML (the /development/ landing and projects index, built by hand). It skips renderContentHTML - the markdown/link-rewrite pipeline that strips the trailing slash off relative links - so card hrefs like "magus/" stay slash-terminated and resolve without a directory redirect. searchText is the plain text indexed for search (the HTML carries no useful prose otherwise).

nodes.label: renderFile
nodes.doc: Render one docs/*.md file to a sibling *.html under gen. rel is relative to the docs/ subtree; srcRel (site.docs/rel) is magus/-relative for the footer/edit link.

nodes.label: renderGraph
nodes.doc: renderGraph writes gen/graph/index.html from the hand-authored graph.html, injecting the shared nav/footer - the graph-explorer analog of renderPlayground. The d3-force bundle (gen/graph/explorer.js) is committed and rebuilt out of band by build-graph-explorer, never by this render; graph.json is the committed demo graph (the magus repo's own knowledge graph). Both sit alongside index.html and are left untouched here (and by pruneStaleOutputs' alwaysKeep set), exactly like the playground's WASM/editor bundl

nodes.label: renderPage
nodes.doc: srcRel is the page's source path relative to magus/ (e.g. "docs/spells.md"); it drives both the per-page commit footer and the "Suggest an edit" link. description is the resolved page description (frontmatter, else derived, else the site default) — always non-empty, so the meta tag always fires. canonUrl is the page's clean gen-root URL ("" for home) for the canonical link. pager is the prev/next nav (may be ""); the home page and lone pages pass "".

nodes.label: renderPager
nodes.doc: renderPager renders the previous/next links among a page's siblings — the other rendered docs in the same source directory, ordered by filename (README/index and underscore-prefixed fixtures excluded). Links are bare basenames, since a sibling lives in the same output directory. "" when there is no sibling on either side. rel is docs-relative (e.g. "modules/fs.md").

nodes.label: renderPagerLink
nodes.doc: renderPagerLink renders one prev/next card, reading the sibling's source so it credits the same title (frontmatter title, else first H1) the sibling page renders with.

nodes.label: renderPlayground
nodes.doc: renderPlayground writes gen/playground/index.html from the hand-authored source, injecting the shared nav + footer at its <!-- site-nav --> / <!-- site-footer --> placeholders. The playground is a standalone page (custom head, wasm, its own scripts) so it does not go through renderPage(); templating only the chrome keeps its top bar and footer in lockstep with every other page. It sits at gen/'s root, so its nav prefix is "".

nodes.label: renderPublic
nodes.doc: renderPublic writes the nested /public/ autoindex tree: the root at /public/ lists atom/ + release/ subdirs plus the machine files that live at their canonical spec locations (/sitemap.xml, /robots.txt); /public/atom/ lists the two Atom feeds; /public/release/ is a placeholder browsing entry (populated in WS U with the release-artifacts matrix). Deterministic (no network at build).

nodes.label: renderReleasesAtom
nodes.doc: renderReleasesAtom writes gen/public/atom/releases.atom.xml from parsed releases (newest first as authored). Per <entry>: title = version, link = GH/releases/tag/<ver>, id = same, updated = release date, summary = the section body. Feed-level updated = newest release's date. Gated on SITE_URL and on having at least one released version.

nodes.label: renderRoot
nodes.doc: Render the repo's top-level README.md as the site landing page (index.html). Links of the form href="docs/X" (relative from the repo root) resolve from gen/index.html as href="X" — strip the docs/ prefix.

nodes.label: renderSiteFooter
nodes.doc: renderSiteFooter renders the shared one-line attribution footer. It leads with two root-relative site links (the Search tool and the /public/ file tree) so those surfaces are reachable from every page, then the absolute GitHub/Buzz links; the License link points at the pinned site.metaBranch (main) like the other GitHub links. root is the relative prefix back to gen/'s root (so the site links resolve at any page depth), matching renderSiteNav's root argument.

nodes.label: renderSiteNav
nodes.doc: navLink renders one top-nav item, marking it aria-current when it is the page the visitor is currently on (key == active). renderSiteNav renders the shared top bar (brand + section links + theme toggle). One definition for every page - docs pages via renderPage(), the 404, and the playground (build-time injected via renderPlayground) - so adding a nav item lands everywhere at once. root is the relative prefix back to gen/'s root; active is the current section's key (or "" for none).

nodes.label: renderSyntaxRow
nodes.doc: renderSearchPage writes gen/search/index.html: the dedicated, deep-linkable search surface. It is a tool page (no markdown source; kept out of the search index and sitemap, and out of the provenance/edit chrome via the srcRel guards in renderPage()). The #search-page container is the signal search.js keys off to switch from the header dropdown to full-page, URL-synced (?q=) results. Emitted after the walk so it is a peer of renderPublic()/render404(). renderSyntaxRow renders one Search Syntax entry: a click

nodes.label: renderToc
nodes.doc: renderToc builds the "On this page" sidebar from a rendered page's h2/h3 headings (goldmark produces each on its own line with a slug id); h3s nest under their h2. Returns "" with fewer than three headings — too little to warrant a sidebar (a module page with one "Methods" h2 over many h3 methods still qualifies) — so renderPage() falls back to a full-width article. toc.js layers scroll-spy + the collapse toggle onto this markup, so the list must already exist for it to find.

nodes.label: renderTourPage
nodes.doc: renderTourPage writes gen/tour/index.html: a numbered card grid that walks a newcomer through magusfiles and Buzz. Each card carries a step number, title, one-liner, a code teaser, and an Open-in-Playground deep-link. A synthesized page like the search page - no .md source - so renderPage()'s srcRel guards drop its provenance/edit chrome.

nodes.label: rootPrefix
nodes.doc: rootPrefix returns the relative path from a generated page back up to gen/'s root ("" for spells.html, "../" for modules/fs.html, "../../" for codes/sandbox/X.html), so the shared nav links and theme.js resolve at any page depth and the site still works when served under a project subpath.

nodes.label: scanReferencesInHTML
nodes.doc: scanReferencesInHTML pulls every href="..." and src="..." out of html, skipping external URLs, mailto:, javascript:, data:, and pure fragments. Each reference is returned as a gen-root-relative path (resolved against pageDir).

nodes.label: site_render
nodes.doc: site_render rebuilds gen/ from ../docs and the root README. Pure function of git history (footers) and the source tree — reproducible and gate-stable.

nodes.label: strLess
nodes.doc: strLess is lexicographic string ordering by byte; buzz has no `<` on str, so the sibling pager spells it out to sort filenames deterministically.

nodes.label: stripTags
nodes.doc: stripTags removes HTML tags from a fragment, leaving its text. The text keeps the HTML-encoded entities goldmark produced, so it drops straight into a double-quoted attribute (TOC link tooltips) without re-escaping.

nodes.label: tourSrc
nodes.doc: tourSrc reads one curated example from the tour/ dir (cwd-relative; build-time only, the files are not written - their content rides into the page as teaser + deep-link).

nodes.label: truncateWords
nodes.doc: truncateWords caps s at about n characters, cutting on a word boundary so the description never ends mid-word. Short paragraphs (the common case) pass through.

nodes.label: updateUrlLock
nodes.doc: updateUrlLock folds this build's URLs into URL_LOCK and rewrites it sorted. It only adds - a URL that drops out of the build stays in the ledger so the gate keeps demanding a redirect or an explicit retirement for it. Called under the rw charm so a plain (checking) render never mutates the tree.

nodes.label: walk
nodes.doc: Recursively walk ../docs (the output lives outside it, under gen).

nodes.label: walkBlog
nodes.doc: walkBlog visits every blog/*.md post file (excluding index.md and _underscored fixtures), in fs.list's sorted order — so the walk is deterministic. Posts are flat: no nested subdirectories under blog/.

nodes.label: walkGen
nodes.doc: walkGen recursively enumerates every written file under gen/ so the integrity check reads each once. Skips buzz.wasm (binary; slow file test suffices).

nodes.label: writeLLMsIndex
nodes.doc: writeLLMsIndex writes gen/llms.txt from the collected docs corpus, mirroring the post-walk search-index.json write. Links are absolute under SITE_URL when set so an external agent can follow them; otherwise root-relative.

nodes.label: writeManifest
nodes.doc: writeManifest writes gen/manifest.webmanifest - the PWA metadata browsers (mostly on Android and Windows) read to allow "install this site". Icons reuse the hat mark (WS A/V); scope/start_url match the deploy base so a nested install stays inside /magus/. Gated on SITE_URL so a same-origin scope is well-defined; without it we'd default to "/" and mis-scope.

nodes.label: writeSEOFiles
nodes.doc: writeSEOFiles writes sitemap.xml + robots.txt from the real page URLs (sorted, so byte-stable), each as an absolute SITE_URL loc — the home page's "index.html" URL maps to the bare site root. Aliases are deliberately absent (only canonical pages belong in a sitemap). Gated on SITE_URL so the site can ship dark until the host is confirmed. robots points crawlers at the sitemap.

nodes.label: writeServiceWorker
nodes.doc: writeServiceWorker writes gen/sw.js - a conservative worker that keeps HTML network-first (never stale docs) and static hashed assets cache-first, and precaches the search index + playground WASM so those two heavy features survive an offline reload. The source lives in website/sw.js.tmpl (Buzz interpolates { ... } inside strings, so embedding JS with arrow functions as inline strings would collide). We swap __VERSION__ (keyed off HEAD's short commit hash so a new build purges old caches on activate) and __

nodes.label: blogDate
nodes.doc: blogDate renders a post's frontmatter date as a plain calendar byline ("July 1, 2026").

nodes.label: blogDateAttr
nodes.doc: blogDateAttr is the date-only "YYYY-MM-DD" that pairs with blogDate() in <time datetime>.

nodes.label: formatISO
nodes.doc: formatISO renders an ISO 8601 UTC string as "June 29, 2026, 1:41 AM UTC" (no-JS fallback; client JS rewrites it to the viewer's local zone).

nodes.label: formatISOShort
nodes.doc: formatISOShort is the compact recent-changes form ("Jan 2, 2006").

nodes.label: attr
nodes.doc: attr renders one escaped attribute with a leading space: ` name="value"`.

nodes.label: el
nodes.doc: el wraps raw body in <tag attrs>...</tag>. attrs is "" or a run of attr() calls.

nodes.label: esc
nodes.doc: esc escapes text for HTML element content (& < >).

nodes.label: voidEl
nodes.doc: voidEl is an unclosed element: <tag attrs> (meta, link, img).

nodes.label: atomDate
nodes.doc: atomDate normalizes a frontmatter date to RFC3339 UTC (bare date -> T00:00:00Z).

nodes.label: escapeXml
nodes.doc: escapeXml is escapeAttr's XML form (five entities, & last to avoid double-encoding).

nodes.label: xmlAttribute
nodes.doc: Minimal XML leaves; every body and value funnels through escapeXml. Container tags with their own namespaces (<feed>, <urlset>, <entry>, <url>) stay written inline.

nodes.label: aliasStub
nodes.doc: aliasStub is the redirect page written at each alias: meta-refresh + canonical + scripted replace + a plain <a>, so it works with or without JavaScript.

nodes.label: cleanBody
nodes.doc: cleanBody reduces a raw markdown body to compact prose for the search index: drop fenced blocks, strip a few inline markers, collapse whitespace.

nodes.label: cleanTarget
nodes.doc: cleanTarget maps an old flat gen-root path to its clean URL: drop .html, drop a trailing index/README landing, drop the "gen" build segment, add a trailing slash.

nodes.label: externalLinks
nodes.doc: externalLinks adds rel/target/data-external to every http(s) anchor that lacks them.

nodes.label: lazyImages
nodes.doc: lazyImages adds loading="lazy" decoding="async" to every without a loading or fetchpriority attribute (author markup wins, so the hero stays eager).

nodes.label: oldFlatDirectory
nodes.doc: oldFlatDirectory is a docs page's pre-move directory (its source subdir).

nodes.label: renderContentHTML
nodes.doc: renderContentHTML is the one post-processing pipeline every page runs: rewrite links, then the ordered content transforms. Routing every render through it keeps a transform from being applied on one path and forgotten on another.

nodes.label: resolvePath
nodes.doc: resolvePath resolves a relative link against a gen-root-relative dir, normalizing ".." and dropping "." / empty segments ("" for the root).

nodes.label: rewriteHref
nodes.doc: rewriteHref rebases one in-content link for the move: resolve against the old flat dir, map to the new clean URL, re-relativize to the new dir. External/anchor/mailto pass through; #fragment preserved; asset (non-.html) links re-based but not cleaned.

nodes.label: rewriteLinks
nodes.doc: rewriteLinks applies rewriteHref to every href="..." in a rendered fragment.

nodes.label: tagAdmonition
nodes.doc:
tagAdmonition turns GitHub-alert blockquotes ("
\n

[!TYPE]\n...") into

callouts. Unknown types pass through.

nodes.label: tagCodeTitle
nodes.doc: tagCodeTitle wraps the <pre> after a "<!-- code-title: NAME -->" marker in a <figure class="code-block-titled"> with a <figcaption>; drops the marker.

nodes.label: tagMermaid
nodes.doc: tagMermaid wraps each mermaid fence in a <div class="mermaid-src"> carrying a no-JS caption (mermaid-init.js renders it to SVG when JS is on).

nodes.label: tagRunnable
nodes.doc: tagRunnable swaps the <pre> after a "<!-- run -->" / "<!-- run-recorder -->" marker with a data-runnable <pre> so run-example.js can find opt-in blocks; drops the marker.

nodes.label: tagRunnableMarker
nodes.doc: tagRunnableMarker replaces the <pre> following each marker with openTag; a marker not immediately before a fence is just dropped.

nodes.label: wrapTables
nodes.doc: wrapTables wraps each rendered <table> in a <figure class="table-scroll"> so a wide table scrolls horizontally within its column instead of overflowing the viewport on narrow screens. goldmark emits a bare <table> that Pico renders full-bleed with no overflow handling of its own.

nodes.label: escapeAttr
nodes.doc: escapeAttr makes s safe inside a double-quoted HTML attribute.

nodes.label: firstPathSegment
nodes.doc: firstPathSegment returns the leading segment of a clean URL ("spells/go/" -> "spells").

nodes.label: pad4
nodes.doc: pad4 zero-pads a small non-negative int to four digits so scores sort lexically.

nodes.label: relativePath
nodes.doc: relativePath returns the relative path from fromDir to toUrl (directory-index layout: a trailing slash means toUrl is a directory). "./" when they resolve to the same place.

nodes.label: sharedTagCount
nodes.doc: sharedTagCount counts tags common to two ||-joined tag strings (relatedness signal).

nodes.label: splitPath
nodes.doc: splitPath splits a gen-root-relative path into non-empty segments, dropping ".".

nodes.label: allDigits
nodes.doc: allDigits reports whether every byte of s is an ASCII digit (and s is non-empty).

nodes.label: blogDateDirectory
nodes.doc: blogDateDirectory turns a YYYY-MM-DD- prefix into nested "YYYY/MM/DD", or null.

nodes.label: blogSlug
nodes.doc: blogSlug drops a leading YYYY-MM-DD- date prefix, leaving the title slug.

nodes.label: blogURLPath
nodes.doc: blogURLPath is a post's URL path below blog/: date scope + slug, or just the slug.

nodes.label: cleanDepth
nodes.doc: cleanDepth is the segment count of a page's clean URL (how many "../" reach root).

nodes.label: cleanURL
nodes.doc: cleanURL maps a docs-relative source path to its clean gen-root URL: drop .md, drop a trailing index/README/_index landing, drop a "gen" build segment, add a slash. Blog posts route through blogURLPath for date scoping. "" for the home page.

nodes.label: hasDatePrefix
nodes.doc: hasDatePrefix detects a leading YYYY-MM-DD- prefix (11 chars) on a slugged filename.

nodes.label: demo
nodes.doc: The one target: it prints values the functions above compute. `run demo` evaluates them for real (the calls run inside the string interpolation), so the trace shows the actual numbers, not placeholders.

nodes.label: fact
nodes.doc: Recursion again, building a product. `eval fact(10)` -> 3628800.

nodes.label: fibo
nodes.doc: Recursion, the classic. `eval fibo(30)` computes 832040 in a real bytecode VM.

nodes.label: reduce
nodes.doc: Higher-order: a function that takes a function, invoked with an inline closure. `eval sum([2, 4, 6, 8])` -> 20.

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: The conventional anchor. Kept to lint here so the plan stays short.

nodes.label: go_lint
nodes.doc: A cheap check. It stays at the default of one slot; several like it run at once.

nodes.label: js_lint
nodes.doc: The greedy one: reserve 4 slots AND tell eslint to actually use 4 threads.

nodes.label: lint
nodes.doc: lint fans out to both. The scheduler runs the 1-slot check alongside other small work, but quiets the queue around js-lint's 4-slot reservation.

nodes.label: release
nodes.doc: A stand-in release, so `graph` shows the exclusive policy in context.

nodes.label: test
nodes.doc: Both of these need build. In the plan it appears once, not twice.

nodes.label: generate
nodes.doc: The umbrella: one glob collects every current AND future *-generate target. glob("name") with no wildcard is shorthand for "anything ending in -name", so glob("generate") would read the same as glob("*-generate") here.

nodes.label: proto_generate
nodes.doc: One target per generator, all sharing the -generate suffix.

nodes.label: proto_lint
nodes.doc: For a pattern a suffix cannot express, reach for regex. proto-all gathers just the proto- family (its generate and its lint), and nothing else.

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: Charms ride down needs() edges, so a single charm on the anchor reaches the whole plan: `run ci:rw` hands rw to format too. Try `run ci`, then `run ci:rw`.

nodes.label: format
nodes.doc: Read the active charm set with has_charm, then branch. (Most real spell ops do this flip internally, so you usually get rw for free; here it is spelled out so the plan shows the difference.)

nodes.label: mgs_getName
nodes.doc: A spell announces its name...

nodes.label: mgs_listTargets
nodes.doc: ...and lists its ops: a map of op name to handler. Each handler takes the Target being built and returns a Command (runs to completion) or a Service.

nodes.label: serve
nodes.doc: serve returns a Service, so magus treats it as a long-running, shared op: command   - the process magus forks in the foreground and supervises readiness - a probe polled until it exits 0 (the Kubernetes exec-probe model), so dependents wait for "actually up", not a fixed sleep stop      - a graceful shutdown, replayed to reap orphans after a crash Reached as a dependency (some target's magus.needs), it runs in the background, shared by config fingerprint; run directly, it runs in the foreground.

nodes.label: build
nodes.doc: Compile the version-stamped server binary and type-check the client.

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: The conventional anchor `magus affected ci` keys off. It declares no work of its own; it fans out to lint, build, and test, which share the format -> generate -> preflight spine and each run once. Try `run ci` to see the plan.

nodes.label: format
nodes.doc: Format every language. These ops CHECK by default; run `magus run format:rw` to APPLY the fixes: the rw charm flips gofmt -l to -w, prettier --check to --write, buf format to -w. The charm rides down from the run, so no branch is needed here. (Try `run format` then `run format:rw` in the console to compare.)

nodes.label: generate
nodes.doc: The umbrella `generate` target pulls in every `*-generate` target with ONE glob instead of listing each by hand: magus.target.glob("*-generate") matches every target ending in -generate, so adding a `sql-generate` later needs no edit here. (For arbitrary patterns there is also magus.target.regex(...).) That is the payoff of naming targets by a shared convention. Try `run generate` to fan out.

nodes.label: image_build
nodes.doc: Build the container image from the compiled binary. Default: a single-arch image loaded into the local daemon. Under the `cd` charm: a multi-arch image pushed to the registry. Kept as its own target so `magus run image-build` works standalone.

nodes.label: ldflags
nodes.doc: A plain Buzz helper that assembles the -ldflags stamping the version into the server binary (used by build below). Try `ldflags(VERSION)` in the console to see Buzz evaluate it; the interpreter is real, only the build tools are stubbed.

nodes.label: lint
nodes.doc: The umbrella `lint` fans out across the whole family with one glob.

nodes.label: preflight
nodes.doc: Gate the pipeline on a healthy workspace before anything builds.

nodes.label: proto_generate
nodes.doc: Codegen is one `*-generate` target per generator. Each is ordinary cached codegen: magus keys it on its inputs and its gen/** outputs, so it reruns only when those inputs change. (Contrast with a drift-gate target that verifies committed output is current on every run; that one would set skipCache because its point is the check, not a cacheable artifact.)

nodes.label: proto_lint
nodes.doc: Linting is a `*-lint` set too, one target per toolchain, so `lint` can glob them the same way `generate` globs its generators. js-lint is split out on its own because it carries the load: eslint is the heavy one, so this target holds LINT_SLOTS slots and runs eslint with a matching --concurrency (the cheap buf/go checks stay at the default of 1 slot). `magus run lint:rw` applies the auto-fixable findings where a tool supports it (e.g. golangci-lint --fix).

nodes.label: release
nodes.doc: Ship a release: a needs() edge onto image-build, so it shows up in `graph`/`ls` as part of the DAG. Charms ride down needs() edges from the run, so `magus run release:cd` flows the cd charm into image-build and it switches to a multi-arch push. (magus.run(["image-build:cd"]) would instead invoke it imperatively with the charm pinned in the call, but an imperative call is not a static edge, so it would not appear in the graph. Both are valid; needs keeps the DAG complete.)

nodes.label: test
nodes.doc: Run both suites.

nodes.label: ci
nodes.doc: The conventional anchor, wired with magus.needs like always.

nodes.label: deploy
nodes.doc: A target can also invoke another imperatively with magus.run - the dynamic alternative to a static magus.needs edge. `run deploy` records the nested call in the plan.

nodes.label: release
nodes.doc: A bespoke phase. It depends on ci through the same magus.needs edge the canonical targets use, then does its own extra work.

nodes.label: stamp
nodes.doc: A target assembling build config from the stdlib the way a real one would: derive an artifact slug and compare two versions, then print them. `run stamp` computes each value for real.

nodes.label: lint
nodes.doc: One op, one base command, two charms that reshape it. `after` splices values in just past an anchor argument; `append` adds to the end. Both resolve to an index now, at author time, so adding a charm later never shifts another's patch.

nodes.label: mgs_listTargets
nodes.doc: A spell lists its ops: a map of op name to handler.

nodes.label: archive.compress
nodes.doc: Create an archive at dest from src (a file or directory). Format is inferred from dest extension (.tar, .tar.gz, .tgz, .tar.zst, .zip). Returns a table with fields: files (archived paths relative to src), bytes_in (raw bytes read), bytes_out (compressed bytes written). opts keys: format (string, override format detection), threads (int, parallel encode workers; 0 or omitted = auto), level (int, compression level; -1 = format default), follow_symlinks (bool, default false), max_size (int, output byte cap, de

nodes.label: archive.uncompress
nodes.doc: Extract the archive at src into dest. Returns a table with fields: files (extracted paths relative to dest) and bytes (total uncompressed bytes written). opts keys: strip (int, strip N leading path components), max_size (int, uncompressed byte cap, default 10 GiB), threads (int, parallel decode workers; 0 or omitted = auto).

nodes.label: charm.after
nodes.doc: Insert vals immediately after the first argv element equal to anchor.

nodes.label: charm.after_func
nodes.doc: Insert vals after the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy.

nodes.label: charm.append
nodes.doc: Append vals to the end of the argv.

nodes.label: charm.before
nodes.doc: Insert vals immediately before the first argv element equal to anchor.

nodes.label: charm.before_func
nodes.doc: Insert vals before the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy.

nodes.label: charm.copy
nodes.doc: Copy the first argv element equal to anchor to the JSON Pointer to ("/-" end, "/0" front, or charm.path(...)).

nodes.label: charm.copy_func
nodes.doc: Copy the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy to the JSON Pointer to.

nodes.label: charm.drop
nodes.doc: Drop (remove) the first argv element equal to anchor.

nodes.label: charm.drop_func
nodes.doc: Drop (remove) the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy.

nodes.label: charm.move
nodes.doc: Move the first argv element equal to anchor to the JSON Pointer to ("/-" end, "/0" front, or charm.path(...)).

nodes.label: charm.move_func
nodes.doc: Move the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy to the JSON Pointer to.

nodes.label: charm.path
nodes.doc: Return the JSON Pointer ("/N") of the first argv element equal to anchor — the index, auto-calculated, for hand-built move/copy/test ops.

nodes.label: charm.path_func
nodes.doc: Return the JSON Pointer ("/N") of the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy.

nodes.label: charm.prepend
nodes.doc: Insert vals at the front of the argv, in order.

nodes.label: charm.set
nodes.doc: Replace the first argv element equal to anchor with val.

nodes.label: charm.set_func
nodes.doc: Replace the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy with val.

nodes.label: charm.test
nodes.doc: Guard: assert the first argv element equal to anchor is still at its position when the patch applies (else the run errors).

nodes.label: charm.test_func
nodes.doc: Guard: assert the first argv element for which fn(s) is truthy is still at its position when the patch applies.

nodes.label: crypto.md5_file
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex MD5 digest of the file at path. For interop with legacy checksum manifests only — MD5 is broken; use sha256 for anything security-relevant.

nodes.label: crypto.md5_hex
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex MD5 digest of data. For interop with legacy checksum manifests only — MD5 is broken; use sha256 for anything security-relevant.

nodes.label: crypto.sha1_file
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-1 digest of the file at path. For interop with legacy/git checksums only — SHA-1 is not collision-resistant; use sha256 for anything security-relevant.

nodes.label: crypto.sha1_hex
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-1 digest of data. For interop with legacy/git checksums only — SHA-1 is not collision-resistant; use sha256 for anything security-relevant.

nodes.label: crypto.sha256_file
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of the file at path.

nodes.label: crypto.sha256_hex
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-256 digest of data.

nodes.label: crypto.sha512_file
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-512 digest of the file at path.

nodes.label: crypto.sha512_hex
nodes.doc: Return the lowercase hex SHA-512 digest of data.

nodes.label: encoding.base64_decode
nodes.doc: Decode a standard (padded) base64 string; errors on malformed input.

nodes.label: encoding.base64_encode
nodes.doc: Encode data as standard (padded) base64.

nodes.label: encoding.base64url_decode
nodes.doc: Decode a URL-safe (padded) base64 string; errors on malformed input.

nodes.label: encoding.base64url_encode
nodes.doc: Encode data as URL-safe (padded) base64.

nodes.label: encoding.build_url
nodes.doc: Build a URL string from a {scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment} map; missing keys are treated as empty.

nodes.label: encoding.hex_decode
nodes.doc: Decode a hex string; errors on malformed input.

nodes.label: encoding.hex_encode
nodes.doc: Encode data as lowercase hex.

nodes.label: encoding.parse_url
nodes.doc: Parse a URL string into {scheme, host, port, path, query, fragment}; errors on malformed input.

nodes.label: encoding.url_decode
nodes.doc: Decode a percent-encoded URL query component; errors on malformed input.

nodes.label: encoding.url_encode
nodes.doc: Percent-encode s for use in a URL query component.

nodes.label: env.expand
nodes.doc: Replace $VAR and ${VAR} references in s with their values (sandbox-stripped names expand to "").

nodes.label: env.get
nodes.doc: Return the value of name, or "" if unset. Use lookup to tell unset from set-but-empty.

nodes.label: env.get_or
nodes.doc: Return the value of name, or def when name is unset or stripped by the sandbox. Unlike get, an empty string is returned as-is — def only applies when the variable is absent.

nodes.label: env.home
nodes.doc: Return the current user's home directory.

nodes.label: env.list
nodes.doc: Return all environment variables as a name→value map.

nodes.label: env.load_dotenv
nodes.doc: Read a .env file and set each variable in the process environment, without overwriting names already set (the dotenv convention) or names the sandbox strips. A no-op in a recording/dry-run.

nodes.label: env.lookup
nodes.doc: Return (value, found); found is false when name is unset or stripped by the sandbox.

nodes.label: env.parse_dotenv
nodes.doc: Parse .env-format content into a name->value map. Supports KEY=VALUE, blank lines, # comments, a leading `export `, single/double quotes (double-quoted values honor \n \t \" \\ escapes), and inline comments after unquoted values. Pure: it does not touch the process environment.

nodes.label: env.read_dotenv
nodes.doc: Read a .env file and return its name->value map (parse_dotenv over the file contents). Errors if the file cannot be read.

nodes.label: env.require
nodes.doc: Return the value of name, or raise when it is unset or stripped by the sandbox. The fail-fast complement to get/lookup: a CI magusfile that needs GITHUB_TOKEN states the requirement once instead of threading a lookup-then-fatal check through every caller. A set-but-empty variable satisfies the requirement (its empty value is returned).

nodes.label: env.set
nodes.doc: Set name to value in the current process environment.

nodes.label: env.unset
nodes.doc: Remove name from the current process environment.

nodes.label: fmt.sprintf
nodes.doc: Format string args into the template using Go printf verbs (e.g. %s, %q). Returns the formatted string.

nodes.label: fs.append_file
nodes.doc: Append content to path (creating if absent, mode 0644).

nodes.label: fs.basename
nodes.doc: Final element of path.

nodes.label: fs.chmod
nodes.doc: Change the permission bits of path to mode (octal integer, e.g. 0755).

nodes.label: fs.copy_dir
nodes.doc: Recursively copy the directory tree at src to dst, preserving permission bits.

nodes.label: fs.copy_file
nodes.doc: Copy the file at src to dst (overwriting), preserving its permission bits.

nodes.label: fs.dirname
nodes.doc: Directory portion of path.

nodes.label: fs.exists
nodes.doc: True iff path exists.

nodes.label: fs.ext
nodes.doc: File-name extension of path, including the leading dot ("" if none).

nodes.label: fs.glob
nodes.doc: Return paths matching pattern (doublestar-style).

nodes.label: fs.is_dir
nodes.doc: True iff path exists and is a directory (a sandbox-denied path reads as false).

nodes.label: fs.is_file
nodes.doc: True iff path exists and is a regular file (a sandbox-denied path reads as false).

nodes.label: fs.join
nodes.doc: Join path elements with the OS separator.

nodes.label: fs.list_dir
nodes.doc: Return directory entries; empty if path does not exist.

nodes.label: fs.mkdirall
nodes.doc: Create path and parents (default mode 0755).

nodes.label: fs.read_file
nodes.doc: Return the contents of path as a string.

nodes.label: fs.read_lines
nodes.doc: Read path and return its lines as a list, with the line terminators stripped. A single trailing newline yields no extra empty element; an empty file yields an empty list.

nodes.label: fs.readlink
nodes.doc: Return the target of the symbolic link at path.

nodes.label: fs.remove_all
nodes.doc: Recursively remove path (no error if missing).

nodes.label: fs.stat
nodes.doc: Return metadata for path as {size, mtime, mode, is_dir}: size in bytes, mtime as Unix millis, mode as the integer permission bits. Errors if path is missing.

nodes.label: fs.symlink
nodes.doc: Create a symbolic link at link pointing to target.

nodes.label: fs.temp_dir
nodes.doc: Create a new temporary directory (in os.TempDir()) with an optional name prefix and return its path.

nodes.label: fs.walk
nodes.doc: Recursively walk the directory tree rooted at root, calling callback(path, is_dir) for each entry. Return true from callback to stop the walk early. Sandbox-denied entries are silently skipped.

nodes.label: fs.watch
nodes.doc: Blocking. Watch paths (directories, recursively) and call callback with each debounced batch of changed paths until the callback returns true or the run is interrupted.

nodes.label: fs.write_file
nodes.doc: Write content to path (mode 0644).

nodes.label: fs.write_lines
nodes.doc: Write lines to path (mode 0644), each followed by a newline. The companion to read_lines: write_lines(p, read_lines(p)) round-trips a newline-terminated file.

nodes.label: http.get
nodes.doc: Send a GET request; returns {status, body, headers}. opts (curl-style): fail, fail_with_body, fail_early (bool); retry (int), retry_delay, retry_max_time, timeout (seconds, default 30); retry_all_errors, retry_connrefused (bool).

nodes.label: http.post
nodes.doc: Send a POST request with body; returns {status, body, headers}. opts (curl-style): fail, fail_with_body, fail_early (bool); retry (int), retry_delay, retry_max_time, timeout (seconds, default 30); retry_all_errors, retry_connrefused (bool).

nodes.label: http.request
nodes.doc: Send an HTTP request; returns {status, body, headers}. opts (curl-style): fail, fail_with_body, fail_early (bool); retry (int), retry_delay, retry_max_time, timeout (seconds, default 30); retry_all_errors, retry_connrefused (bool).

nodes.label: http.server
nodes.doc: Start a static file server for dir in the background and return the bound port. With no port (or 0) it scans upward from 8080 and binds the first available port. Serves localhost only and runs until the process exits, so pair it with a blocking call like fs.watch.

nodes.label: json.parse
nodes.doc: Decode a JSON string into a value (map, list, string, number, or boolean).

nodes.label: json.stringify
nodes.doc: Encode a value as a JSON string. With no indent (or "") the output is compact; pass an indent string (e.g. "  " or "\t") for pretty, multi-line output.

nodes.label: magus.bust_cache
nodes.doc: Invalidate the build cache. Escape hatch — prefer modeling missing inputs as Sources. No arg clears all; a project path clears one project.

nodes.label: magus.cmd
nodes.doc: Escape hatch: run `magus <args>` for any subcommand, in the target's project directory. Prefer the dedicated methods (run, describe, insight, doctor) when one exists — magus.cmd warns when args name a subcommand that has one. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit (catch for non-fatal use). opts.root sets the global --root workspace; opts.quiet captures the output without echoing it to the console.

nodes.label: magus.describe
nodes.doc: Run `magus describe <args>` in the target's project directory and capture its output. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit (catch for non-fatal use). opts.root sets the global --root workspace; opts.quiet captures the output without echoing it to the console. Unlike a raw binary call, the working directory is always the contextual project dir, so a nested project describes itself, not the root workspace.

nodes.label: magus.doctor
nodes.doc: Run `magus doctor <args>` in the target's project directory and capture its output. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit (catch for non-fatal use). opts.root sets the global --root workspace; opts.quiet captures the output without echoing it to the console.

nodes.label: magus.has_charm
nodes.doc: True when execution charm `name` is active, letting a target body branch on a charm carried in context (e.g. has_charm("rw")).

nodes.label: magus.insight
nodes.doc: Run `magus insight <args>` in the target's project directory and capture its output. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit (catch for non-fatal use). opts.root sets the global --root workspace; opts.quiet captures the output without echoing it to the console.

nodes.label: magus.run
nodes.doc: Run `magus run <args>` recursively in the target's project directory and capture its output. Child invocations share the parent's concurrency budget over the local socket. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit (catch for non-fatal use). opts.root sets the global --root workspace; opts.quiet captures the output without echoing it to the console.

nodes.label: markdown.frontmatter
nodes.doc: Parse the leading YAML frontmatter block (a "---" fenced header at the top of the document) and return it as a JSON object string; decode with serialize.jsonDecode. Returns "{}" when no frontmatter is present.

nodes.label: markdown.strip_frontmatter
nodes.doc: Return the Markdown body with any leading YAML frontmatter block removed (the source unchanged when none is present).

nodes.label: markdown.to_html
nodes.doc: Render GitHub-Flavored Markdown to semantic HTML. Strips a leading YAML frontmatter block (a "---" fenced header at the top of the document) before rendering. Auto-IDs every heading so #fragment links resolve, and rewrites relative .md links (incl. README.md → index.html) to their generated .html equivalents. Raw HTML in the source is passed through (intended for trusted, first-party docs).

nodes.label: os.exec
nodes.doc: Run cmd directly (no shell; args are never shell-interpolated). Output streams live and is captured. Returns {stdout, stderr, code, ok}; raises on non-zero exit unless opts.allow_failure is true. Optional dir runs cmd there (relative to the target's cwd). opts.stdin is fed to the process as standard input — pipe by passing a prior call's stdout.

nodes.label: os.exec_sh
nodes.doc: Run line through a shell — for pipes, redirection, globs, and variable expansion. Default shell is /bin/sh (cmd on Windows); pass opts.shell (e.g. "bash") to override, resolved via PATH. A shell line is written in the platform shell's dialect, so sh and cmd lines are not portable across OSes — for cross-platform logic prefer os.exec plus the fs/os helpers. Same result and raise semantics as exec (opts.stdin and opts.allow_failure included); optional dir runs the shell there.

nodes.label: os.exit
nodes.doc: Abort the current run with the given exit code — typically after logging an error. Does NOT call os.Exit (that would kill a shared daemon); it raises, ending the target, and the code becomes magus's process exit status.

nodes.label: os.hostname
nodes.doc: Return the host machine's name.

nodes.label: os.num_cpu
nodes.doc: Return the number of logical CPUs available, for sizing a command's own internal parallelism (see os.with_slots).

nodes.label: os.platform
nodes.doc: Return the Docker/OCI platform triple: (os, arch, variant).

nodes.label: os.retry
nodes.doc: Call fn up to max times, retrying on error with exponential backoff; returns fn's value on success. opts: {backoff_ms:float (default 500), max_backoff_ms:float (default 30000)}.

nodes.label: os.sleep
nodes.doc: Pause for the given number of milliseconds (fractional allowed), matching Buzz's os.sleep. Cancellable: if the run is interrupted it returns early with the cancellation error rather than blocking.

nodes.label: os.stdin_is_terminal
nodes.doc: Report whether standard input is a terminal (TTY) rather than a pipe, file, or /dev/null. Use it to fail fast with a clear message instead of blocking on a read of stdin that will never receive piped input.

nodes.label: os.which
nodes.doc: Resolve cmd against PATH and return its absolute path, or "" if it is not found. Use it to check a tool is installed before running it (and emit a clear hint/error instead of a cryptic exec failure).

nodes.label: os.with_env
nodes.doc: Set env vars for the duration of callback; restore after.

nodes.label: os.with_slots
nodes.doc: Reserve n slots from magus's concurrency budget for the duration of callback. Use when callback runs a command with its own internal parallelism (make -j, a test runner) that magus can't see, so the global budget is not oversubscribed.

nodes.label: path.abs
nodes.doc: Return the absolute form of path, resolved against the current directory and lexically cleaned.

nodes.label: path.clean
nodes.doc: Return the shortest lexically-equivalent path (resolves . and .., collapses separators).

nodes.label: path.expand_user
nodes.doc: Expand a leading ~ (or ~/...) to the current user's home directory; other paths are returned unchanged.

nodes.label: path.is_abs
nodes.doc: Report whether path is absolute.

nodes.label: path.rel
nodes.doc: Return a relative path from base to target; errors if no relative path exists.

nodes.label: platform.arch
nodes.doc: Normalize an architecture identifier (x86_64, aarch64, armv7l, …) to canonical Go GOARCH (amd64, arm64, arm). With style, render that result in a convention (go|uname); raises on an unknown style. Returns "" when the identifier is unrecognized.

nodes.label: platform.os
nodes.doc: Normalize an OS identifier (Darwin, macOS, win, …) to canonical Go GOOS (darwin, windows). With style, render that result in a convention (go|uname); raises on an unknown style. Returns "" when the identifier is unrecognized.

nodes.label: semver.compare
nodes.doc: Compare two semver strings; op is "==", "!=", "<", "<=", ">", or ">=" — true when the relation holds.

nodes.label: semver.parse
nodes.doc: Parse a semver string into {major, minor, patch, prerelease, metadata, original}; errors on invalid input.

nodes.label: strings.camel_case
nodes.doc: Convert s to camelCase.

nodes.label: strings.capitalize
nodes.doc: Uppercase the first rune of s and lowercase the rest.

nodes.label: strings.ellipsis
nodes.doc: Trim s to at most length runes, appending "..." when truncated.

nodes.label: strings.kebab_case
nodes.doc: Convert s to kebab-case.

nodes.label: strings.pascal_case
nodes.doc: Convert s to PascalCase.

nodes.label: strings.snake_case
nodes.doc: Convert s to snake_case.

nodes.label: strings.words
nodes.doc: Split s into its constituent words (splitting on case changes, digits, and separators).

nodes.label: template.render
nodes.doc: Render a Mustache template against a context value (usually a name->value map; lists drive sections, absent/false keys hide them). Returns the filled string; errors on a malformed template.

nodes.label: template.render_partials
nodes.doc: Render a Mustache template that includes partials via {{>name}}, resolving each name against the partials map (name->template string). Partials may reference other partials. Same context and escaping rules as render; errors on a malformed template.

nodes.label: time.add
nodes.doc: Add a Go duration string (e.g. "24h", "-1h30m") to a Unix-millis timestamp; returns the new Unix-millis timestamp.

nodes.label: time.diff
nodes.doc: Return a minus b in milliseconds (positive when a is later than b).

nodes.label: time.format
nodes.doc: Render Unix-millis as a string using a Go reference layout (UTC).

nodes.label: time.now_iso
nodes.doc: Return the current UTC time as an RFC 3339 string. For the raw epoch-millis value use Buzz's os.time().

nodes.label: time.parse
nodes.doc: Parse a string with a Go reference layout into Unix-millis (UTC); errors on mismatch.

nodes.label: time.parse_duration
nodes.doc: Parse a Go duration string (e.g. "168h", "1h30m") into milliseconds; errors on mismatch.

nodes.label: toml.parse
nodes.doc: Decode a TOML document into a value (tables become maps, arrays become lists, plus strings, numbers, bools, and datetimes); errors on invalid input.

nodes.label: toml.stringify
nodes.doc: Encode a value to a TOML string; the top level must be a table/map, as TOML requires. Errors on unencodable input.

nodes.label: uuid.randomHex
nodes.doc: A cryptographically random lowercase hex string of n bytes (2*n characters); errors when n is not positive.

nodes.label: uuid.randomToken
nodes.doc: A cryptographically random URL-safe base64 token from n bytes of entropy (no padding); errors when n is not positive.

nodes.label: uuid.v4
nodes.doc: A random (version 4) UUID string, e.g. "9b1deb4d-3b7d-4bad-9bdd-2b0d7b3dcb6d".

nodes.label: uuid.v7
nodes.doc: A time-ordered (version 7) UUID string; lexically sorts by creation time, which makes it a good ordered run/build id.

nodes.label: vcs.branch
nodes.doc: Current branch, or empty on error.

nodes.label: vcs.commit
nodes.doc: Resolve a revision (a VCS-native rev expression; omit for the current revision) to its commit record: {id, short, author {name, email}, date, subject, body, parents}. id is the content/revision id (git SHA, hg node, jj commit_id); date is RFC3339, when the revision was recorded. Every field is meaningful for every VCS. Returns the zero record (every field empty) when no VCS is resolved or the revision can't be looked up — test a field (e.g. c.date == "") rather than for null.

nodes.label: vcs.commit_date
nodes.doc: Commit date string, or empty on error.

nodes.label: vcs.describe
nodes.doc: Human-readable version string from the nearest tag (git's `describe --tags --always --dirty`: tag, else short hash, with a -dirty suffix for a modified tree). "" when no VCS is resolved, or for a backend without a tag-describe concept (jj) — so a magusfile stamps a version without shelling out to git. Pair with vcs.shortHash() as a fallback.

nodes.label: vcs.diff
nodes.doc: List files changed against the given base (defaults to vcs.base).

nodes.label: vcs.exe
nodes.doc: Absolute path to the active VCS executable (git/hg/jj), or "" if unresolved. Lets a magusfile run a VCS-agnostic escape-hatch command: os.exec(vcs.exe(), [...]).

nodes.label: vcs.hash
nodes.doc: Full commit hash, or empty on error.

nodes.label: vcs.history
nodes.doc: Up to limit recent commits, newest first; each is the same record vcs.commit returns. limit defaults to 10 when omitted. An empty list when no VCS is resolved.

nodes.label: vcs.is_dirty
nodes.doc: True if the working tree has uncommitted changes. Pass paths to scope the check to those files/dirs (relative to the project), e.g. is_dirty(["MAGUS.md"]) — the right way to gate generated outputs without shelling out to git or parsing porcelain.

nodes.label: vcs.metadata
nodes.doc: Full metadata table: short_hash, hash, branch, commit_date, is_dirty.

nodes.label: vcs.root
nodes.doc: Absolute path of the repository root.

nodes.label: vcs.short_hash
nodes.doc: Short commit hash, or empty on error.

nodes.label: yaml.parse
nodes.doc: Decode a YAML string into a value (maps, lists, strings, numbers, bools, null); errors on invalid input.

nodes.label: yaml.stringify
nodes.doc: Encode a value to a YAML string; errors on unencodable input.

nodes.label: archive
nodes.doc: Archive creation and extraction with automatic format detection. Supports tar, zip, tar.gz, tar.bz2, tar.xz, and tar.zst. Symlinks and non-regular entries are skipped.

nodes.label: charm
nodes.doc: Constructors for charm values: RFC 6902 JSON Patches over a target's argv (see docs/charms.md).

nodes.label: crypto
nodes.doc: Content digests (SHA-256/512; SHA-1 and MD5 for legacy-checksum interop).

nodes.label: encoding
nodes.doc: Base64/hex/URL text codecs.

nodes.label: env
nodes.doc: Process environment variable access.

nodes.label: fmt
nodes.doc: String formatting (printf-style).

nodes.label: fs
nodes.doc: Filesystem and path primitives.

nodes.label: http
nodes.doc: HTTP client with automatic retry on transient errors.

nodes.label: json
nodes.doc: JSON encode/decode.

nodes.label: magus
nodes.doc: Magus core primitives.

nodes.label: markdown
nodes.doc: GitHub-Flavored Markdown to semantic HTML.

nodes.label: os
nodes.doc: Process execution. os.exec runs a command directly (no shell); os.exec_sh runs a line through the shell. Both stream output live and return a result {stdout, stderr, code, ok}.

nodes.label: path
nodes.doc: Pure path-string math: abs, rel, clean, is_abs, expand_user.

nodes.label: platform
nodes.doc: Normalize OS/architecture identifiers across naming conventions (aarch64↔arm64, Darwin↔darwin).

nodes.label: semver
